info.name: Expense Splitter API
info.description:
Complete collection for the Expense Splitter backend (Go + Echo + Postgres + Keycloak + MinIO).

## Quick start
1. `docker compose up --build -d` (server on http://localhost:8080), then seed once: `docker compose exec server ./server seed`.
2. **Public / Auth -> Login** — stores the token; every request inherits it.
3. Run folders top to bottom: registered users, verification, groups, expenses, close, payments.

## Chained variables (set automatically by test scripts)
- Login -> `access_token` | Register -> `memberUserId` | Get me -> `userId`
- Create group -> `groupId`, `inviteToken` | Get group -> `statusToken`
- Record expense -> `expenseId` | Get settlement plan -> `paymentId`

## Roles cheat-sheet
- Seed **global admin**: `admin@expense-splitter.local` / `admin` (cannot be a trip member; creates groups only via `admin_user_id`).
- **Group admin**: creator of the group (or assigned member); approves joins, closes, finalizes payments.
- **Member**: records own expenses, submits proof for what they owe, confirms what they are owed.

item.name: 0. E2E — run this folder in the Collection Runner

request.description: Start of the end-to-end story. Run the WHOLE folder via Collection Runner (right-click folder -> Run).
Creates the trip's payer.
Creates the trip's debtor.
Global admin verifies the payer's account.
Global admin verifies the debtor's account.
Payer session.
Debtor session.
Payer becomes group-admin.
Captures the public share token.
Debtor requests to join via invite token.
Join request approved.
Payer paid 10.000 OMR for everyone.
Payer paid 9.000 OMR, split 2:1 (payer:debtor).
Pagination envelope check.
Payer net must be +8.000 OMR (paid 19000, fair 11000).
The only unauthenticated view.
Freeze + settle.
One pending payment debtor -> payer of 8.000 OMR.
Must be DENIED (403) — the tamper-resistance spine.
First key: the debtor's evidence.
Second key: the creditor attests receipt.
Third key: group-admin settles it.
Aggregate 1 of 1 + group flipped to settled.
The full story, recorded.
The final artifact.
Public liveness + DB ping.
Public signup: creates the user in Keycloak and the local row (status `registered`). Stores {{memberUserId}}. A fresh email is generated on every run so it never 409s.

Password login proxied to Keycloak. Returns tokens AND your user (the local account is auto-provisioned/linked on login — no separate register-link step needed). Stores {{access_token}}, {{refresh_token}}, {{userId}}. Access tokens last ~5 min: use Refresh, not re-login.

exec: if (j && j.user && j.user.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('userId', j.user.id); console.log('logged in as ' + j.user.email); }

request.description: Same login, but with the credentials of the user created by 'Register (sign up)'. OVERWRITES the collection token — every request now runs as that member. Re-run the admin Login to switch back.

exec: if (j && j.user) { console.log('now acting as ' + j.user.email + ' (' + j.user.verification_status + ')'); }

request.description: Trades {{refresh_token}} for a fresh pair — renew the session without re-sending the password. Updates both stored tokens.
Revokes the Keycloak session behind {{refresh_token}} (idempotent — 204 even if already invalid). The refresh token stops working immediately; the short-lived access token expires on its own.
The ONLY unauthenticated group view: name, status, total spent, member count — nothing else.
Token claims + linked local account. Stores {{userId}} (your own id).

exec: if (j && j.local_user && j.local_user.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('userId', j.local_user.id); console.log('userId = ' + j.local_user.id); }

request.description: OPTIONAL/legacy: login now auto-provisions the local account. Kept as an idempotent manual link for tools that only have a bearer token.
registered -> pending_verification. The global admin then approves/rejects.
Global admin, PAGINATED: {total, limit, offset, items}. Enable status/limit/offset params to filter and page.
Global admin: one account plus every group membership it holds.
Global admin: pending_verification (or registered) -> verified.
Global admin: -> rejected.
Global admin. Guards: not yourself, not the global admin, and 409 for anyone who belongs to groups (memberships anchor expenses/payments/audit). Audited as user.deleted.
Global admin, PAGINATED envelope {total, limit, offset, items}: every group with status, member count and total spent.
Global admin. Only pristine groups (no expenses, payments or audit history) can be deleted — everything else is immutable bookkeeping. Audited as group.deleted.
Groups the caller belongs to, with their role per group.
The creator becomes group-admin. Do NOT send admin_user_id as a normal user. Stores {{groupId}} and {{inviteToken}}.

exec: if (j && j.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('groupId', j.id); console.log('groupId = ' + j.id); }

item.name: Create group (as global admin — assign member)

request.description: The global admin is not a trip member: they must assign a DIFFERENT verified user as group-admin via admin_user_id ({{memberUserId}} — register + approve one first).

exec: if (j && j.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('groupId', j.id); console.log('groupId = ' + j.id); }

request.description: Metadata + member list. Stores {{statusToken}} (the public share token).
Group-admin, open groups only. Replaces the editable metadata.
Group-admin. Freezes expenses, computes the min-transfer plan, creates pending payments. A plan with zero transfers settles the group immediately.
Verified user requests to join via the shareable invite token.
Group-admin: pending requests. Stores {{memberUserId}} (first requester).
Group-admin approves the join request.
Group-admin rejects the join request.
Transfers the group-admin role (exactly one admin at all times; audited).
Group-admin removes a member, or a member removes themself. 409 if they recorded expenses or appear in any expense split.
paid_by is always the caller. Categories: lodging, fuel, food, transport, other. occurred_on must be inside the trip dates. Stores {{expenseId}}.

exec: if (j && j.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('expenseId', j.id); console.log('expenseId = ' + j.id); }

request.description: Optional shares[] limits the split to named APPROVED members; weight scales their portion (weight 2 owes twice weight 1). Omit shares for an equal split.

exec: if (j && j.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('expenseId', j.id); console.log('expenseId = ' + j.id); }

request.description: PAGINATED envelope {total, limit, offset, items}. Descriptions longer than 80 chars are truncated on a word boundary. Enable params to filter and page.
Own expense, open group. An amount change is audited with before/after. Shares are immutable.
Soft-deletes your own expense while the group is open. Audited.
Totals, spend per category, and per member paid / fair share / net. Works open or closed; weighted splits are reflected.

The who-pays-whom plan for a closed group: per-payment status, the aggregate (settled_count of total_count), and the settlement snapshot (total spent, member count, each member's paid / fair share / net). An empty plan carries a note explaining why (e.g. single member, or all nets zero). Stores {{paymentId}}.

exec: console.log(j.settled_count + ' of ' + j.total_count + ' settled | paymentId = ' + j.payments[0].id);

console.warn('PLAN IS EMPTY: ' + (j.note || 'no payments') + ' — {{paymentId}} was NOT set, so folder 9 requests will fail with invalid payment id. You need a group with 2+ members where someone overpaid (or run `make demo`).');

request.description: PDF for a FULLY-settled group: balances, expenses, plan with who confirmed/finalized. Use 'Send and Download' to save it. 409 before fully settled.
DEBTOR only: pending|disputed -> proof_submitted. Log in as the payment's from-user.

DEBTOR only, multipart upload. The file must be a REAL image (magic-byte check: jpeg/png/gif/webp, max 5 MiB) — renamed binaries are rejected. sha256 + size stored; bytes go to MinIO. Pick a file in the Body tab before sending.
Current proof: type, size, sha256, note (for text proofs). Payment parties + admins only.
The stored image itself. 409 when the proof is a text note.
CREDITOR only: proof_submitted -> creditor_confirmed.
CREDITOR only: 'I did not receive this' -> disputed (debtor re-submits proof).
GROUP-ADMIN (from creditor_confirmed only) or GLOBAL ADMIN (override, any non-settled step) -> settled. A debtor can NEVER finalize their own payment. Last settle flips the group to fully settled.
GROUP-ADMIN or GLOBAL ADMIN -> disputed (back to the debtor).
Group-admin/global-admin, PAGINATED envelope: full trail — who, what, before/after, when.
Group-admin: reminds debtors on stale pending payments and creditors on stale proof_submitted ones. Idempotent — re-running inside the threshold sends nothing.
